source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A circular converging lens, with diameter \( d = 32 \, \mathrm{mm} \) and focal length \( f = 24 \, \mathrm{cm} \), forms images of distant point objects in the focal plane of the lens. The wavelength is \( \lambda = 550 \, \mathrm{nm} \). What is the separation \( \Delta x \) of the centers of the image in the focal plane? (That is, what is the separation of the central peaks in the two intensity-versus-position curves?)

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is intended to rely on the image
The text provides lens diameter, focal length, and wavelength but appears to omit an additional needed geometric quantity for the separation, suggesting key information is likely only in the image
image_manifest shows 1 available image and registry_passed=true, so the visual asset is present for use
multi_step: Solving likely requires extracting a visual/geometric parameter from the image and then applying an optics relation, so it is at least a two-step process
The problem combines multiple physics quantities (diameter, focal length, wavelength) rather than asking for direct lookup
Multiple-choice format does not by itself increase reasoning depth, so the score is moderate rather than very high
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a concrete numeric answer ('5.0 μm') is straightforward to check
choices are provided, enabling easy answer matching against option B
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true support reliable downstream verification

decision_reasons: registry_passed=true with no listed issues, and the core assets are present: question, answer, and one image
The sample is strongly image-dependent (image_dependency_score=0.93), and the required visual asset exists
The answer is concrete and easy to verify (verifiability_score=0.89; short_text answer present)
Although risk flags note unit tolerance and a visual parameter not explicit in text, these do not remove core semantic recoverability because the image is available
